location: Found in a chest inside a wagon/carriage on the western road out of Gatefront Ruins, just southeast of the Gatefront Site of Grace, in Limgrave.
tactics: The weapon is inside a chest in the back of a horse-drawn wagon, not lying on open ground — easy to walk right past if you don't check the carriage.
A hostile soldier guards the wagon; clear it before climbing in to loot the chest.
No boss fight or key required — it's a simple early-game pickup right off the main road.
Its unusually high 110 critical modifier (versus the ~100 most Greatswords have) makes it worth grabbing early if you like backstabs or parries.
lore: A well-made, standard-issue blade once carried by rank-and-file soldiers of a lord's army. Age and battle have blackened and scarred it, though it's stayed serviceable even as the men who once wielded it have long since lost their minds.

builds.name: Crit/Parry Quality build
builds.why: The above-average 110 critical modifier makes backstabs and riposte damage hit noticeably harder than most Greatswords, while still supporting any Ash of War infusion.
